ARGUED. The Court hears arguments on both demurrers together.
Ms. Wallis contests and presents arguments; complaint unclear, cites case law, distinction of ultimate facts and legal conclusion on alter ego, doctrine of less particularity.

Mr. Klingsporn submits to the TR.
The Court extends one pass to counsel on the late notice to contest TR.
Ms. Wallis thanks the court for the professional courtesy; contests and presents argument; cites case authority, new amendment will open new area of discovery,bringing in another defendant extremely prejudicial.
Mr. Klingsporn responds; objects to late TR, gives background/context, client former founder then cut out, alter ego issue, assertion of prejudice, not stated, opposing counsel gave no reason to stipulate to a SAC.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff Sam Zaid's Motion To File Second Amended Complaint.

Taken Under Submission.***

APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
Off Calendar Jun-13-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
GREGORY K. KLINGSPORN, Esq., of JORGENSON, SIEGEL, McCLURE & FLEGEL, LLP. Tel: (650) 324-9300
for plaintiff.
Georgiana A. Nikias, Esq., of MUNCK WILSON MANDALA, LLP Tel:(310) 855-3311 for defendant.
Court Reporter: Victoria Thomas CSR# 12927 Tel:559.623.3367 email. VLThomasCSR@gmail.com.

Hearing is held. Plaintiff counsel contest the tentative ruling denying his application for a writ of attachment against defendant Getaround, Inc. line #10 of Friday's law and motion calendar, case CGC25622586. Specifically the portion of the tentative ruling stating: "Mr. Zaid has not shown that any of his claims are probably valid or that the amount he seeks to have secured by the attachment is fixed or readily ascertainable. . The matter argued. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Sam Zaid's application for a right to attach order and issuance of a writ of attachment is denied. Mr. Zaid has not shown that any of his claims are probably valid or that the amount he seeks to have secured by the attachment is fixed or readily ascertainable.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Court Reporter: Victoria Thomas CSR# 12927 Tel:559.623.3367 email. VLThomasCSR@gmail.com. Reported. (302/HEK)
